1	# Delivery Acceptance and Project Architecture Audit (Static-Only)
2	
3	Date: 2026-04-10
4	Scope: repository root only, static analysis only
5	
6	## 1. Verdict
7	- Overall conclusion: **Partial Pass**
8	
9	Rationale:
10	- The repository is substantial and production-shaped (modular services, migrations, Docker deployment, RBAC, audit trails).
11	- Multiple **material requirement-fit gaps** and **high-risk implementation issues** remain, especially around tenant isolation, notification retry semantics, backup robustness, and requirement alignment details.
12	
13	## 2. Scope and Static Verification Boundary
14	### What was reviewed
15	- Documentation and run/test/config references: `README.md`, `docs/manual-verification.md`, `.env.example`, `Makefile`, `deploy/docker-compose.yml`, `deploy/Dockerfile`
16	- Entry points and route wiring: `cmd/api/main.go`, `internal/app/app.go`, `internal/app/routes.go`
17	- Security/authz and middleware: `internal/http/middleware.go`, `internal/auth/*`, `internal/*/routes.go`, handlers/services for work orders/payments/governance/attachments/admin
18	- Data model/migrations: `db/migrations/*.up.sql`
19	- Static test corpus: `internal/**/*_test.go`, `test/*_test.go`
20	
21	### What was not reviewed
22	- Runtime behavior under actual MySQL/Docker/network conditions
23	- Live performance, latency, concurrency, and backup restore execution outcomes
24	
25	### What was intentionally not executed
26	- No project start, no Docker, no tests, no migrations, no external services
27	
28	### Claims requiring manual verification
29	- p95 latency under 300 ms at 50 RPS (cannot prove statically)
30	- Effective behavior of anomaly allowlist when deployed behind proxies/load balancers
31	- Real backup/restore correctness against production-sized datasets
32	- End-to-end scheduled jobs timing and operational behavior in long-running process
33	
34	## 3. Repository / Requirement Mapping Summary
35	- Prompt core goal: offline-first single-node PropertyOps backend for maintenance, governance, notifications, payments, analytics, with strict RBAC/security/audit constraints.
36	- Mapped implementation areas: auth/session, RBAC middleware, work orders and SLA/dispatch, attachments, governance and enforcements, payments/reconciliation, notifications/messaging, analytics/export, admin/ops, backups, migrations, and tests.
37	- Main shortfalls: requirement-fit gaps in maintenance image submission semantics, tag search analytics semantics, retry logic behavior, and tenant boundary enforcement on work-order creation.
38	
39	## 4. Section-by-section Review
40	
41	### 4.1 Hard Gates
42	#### 4.1.1 Documentation and static verifiability
43	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
44	- Rationale: Main README and config docs are strong, but manual verification docs contain endpoint/response mismatches that reduce static trustability.
45	- Evidence:
46	  - `README.md:1`
47	  - `docs/manual-verification.md:31` (expects wrapped success/data for health)
48	  - `internal/health/handler.go:39` (actual liveness response is plain `{status:"ok"}`)
49	  - `docs/manual-verification.md:110` (uses `/api/v1/admin/backups/run`)
50	  - `internal/backups/routes.go:22` (actual backup endpoint is `POST /api/v1/admin/backups`)
51	- Manual verification note: Human reviewer should treat manual guide endpoint/response examples as partially stale.
52	
53	#### 4.1.2 Material deviation from prompt
54	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
55	- Rationale: System is largely aligned, but there are notable deviations/weak fits:
56	  - Work-order image support is implemented as separate attachment uploads, not in request submission payload.
57	  - Tag analysis uses only `skill_tag`, not search-term analysis over work-order tags JSON.
58	  - Notification retry behavior appears under-implemented.
59	- Evidence:
60	  - `internal/workorders/dto.go:9` (create payload has no image list)
61	  - `internal/attachments/routes.go:23` (separate upload route)
62	  - `internal/analytics/repository.go:129` (tag analysis on `skill_tag` only)
63	  - `internal/app/scheduler.go:95`-`internal/app/scheduler.go:111` (delivery SQL does not use per-notification retry mutation methods)
64	
65	### 4.2 Delivery Completeness
66	#### 4.2.1 Core explicit requirements coverage
67	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
68	- Rationale:
69	  - Implemented: auth, roles, sessions, work-order lifecycle, SLA computation, dispatch, attachments with MIME/signature/size/count checks, governance queue/actions, payments intents/postings/approvals/reconciliation, analytics, admin ops, backups, health/log APIs.
70	  - Incomplete/weak: tenant ownership validation on work-order creation, tag-search semantics, robust retry flow semantics.
71	- Evidence:
72	  - `internal/workorders/service.go:63` (SLA and creation logic)
73	  - `internal/workorders/service.go:500` (SLA windows)
74	  - `internal/attachments/service.go:49`-`internal/attachments/service.go:50` (6 attachments)
75	  - `internal/attachments/service.go:95`-`internal/attachments/service.go:140` (size/MIME/signature)
76	  - `internal/payments/service.go:61` (intent creation with expiry)
77	  - `internal/payments/service.go:366` (dual approval)
78	  - `internal/governance/service.go:44`-`internal/governance/service.go:64` (report categories)
79	  - `internal/workorders/handler.go:35` + `internal/workorders/service.go:131` (missing tenant-property/unit ownership validation before create)
80	
81	#### 4.2.2 End-to-end 0-to-1 deliverable shape
82	- Conclusion: **Pass**
83	- Rationale: Full multi-module repository with migrations, deployment manifests, command entry points, docs, and tests (unit + integration).
84	- Evidence:
85	  - `cmd/api/main.go:1`
86	  - `db/migrations/000001_create_users_and_roles.up.sql:1` through `db/migrations/000010_create_admin_ops.up.sql:1`
87	  - `deploy/docker-compose.yml:1`
88	  - `README.md:1`
89	  - `test/setup_test.go:1`
90	
91	### 4.3 Engineering and Architecture Quality
92	#### 4.3.1 Structure and module decomposition
93	- Conclusion: **Pass**
94	- Rationale: Clear separation by domain modules with centralized route wiring and shared middleware/services.
95	- Evidence:
96	  - `internal/app/routes.go:65`-`internal/app/routes.go:158`
97	  - `internal/*` modular package layout
98	
99	#### 4.3.2 Maintainability/extensibility
100	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
101	- Rationale: Mostly maintainable, but several scheduler operations use direct SQL side effects that bypass service-layer invariants and make extension/testing harder.
102	- Evidence:
103	  - `internal/app/scheduler.go:92`-`internal/app/scheduler.go:177`
104	
105	### 4.4 Engineering Details and Professionalism
106	#### 4.4.1 Error handling, logging, validation, API design
107	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
108	- Rationale:
109	  - Strong: centralized error envelope, validation helpers, structured request logging, role middleware.
110	  - Weak points: some high-risk logic paths are permissive or not fully aligned to requirement semantics.
111	- Evidence:
112	  - `internal/common/errors.go:1`
113	  - `internal/common/validators.go:1`
114	  - `internal/http/middleware.go:57`-`internal/http/middleware.go:207`
115	  - `internal/logs/logger.go:1`
116	
117	#### 4.4.2 Real product vs demo
118	- Conclusion: **Pass**
119	- Rationale: This is a real service-shaped backend with persistence, migration strategy, security mechanisms, and operational endpoints.
120	- Evidence:
121	  - `README.md:1`
122	  - `deploy/docker-compose.yml:1`
123	  - `internal/app/app.go:1`
124	
125	### 4.5 Prompt Understanding and Requirement Fit
126	#### 4.5.1 Business semantics and constraint fit
127	- Conclusion: **Partial Pass**
128	- Rationale: Major business capabilities are represented, but several semantics are only partial/misaligned (image-on-submission flow, tag search interpretation, retry behavior, tenant boundary on creation).
129	- Evidence:
130	  - `internal/workorders/dto.go:9`
131	  - `internal/attachments/routes.go:23`
132	  - `internal/analytics/repository.go:129`
133	  - `internal/app/scheduler.go:95`
134	  - `internal/workorders/service.go:131`
135	
136	### 4.6 Aesthetics (frontend-only/full-stack)
137	- Conclusion: **Not Applicable**
138	- Rationale: Backend-only repository; no frontend/UI deliverable in scope.
139	
140	## 5. Issues / Suggestions (Severity-Rated)
141	
142	### Blocker / High
143	
144	1) **High** - Tenant can create work orders for arbitrary properties/units (tenant isolation boundary gap)
145	- Conclusion: **Fail**
146	- Evidence:
147	  - `internal/workorders/handler.go:35` (role check only)
148	  - `internal/workorders/service.go:131` (direct create)
149	  - `internal/workorders/dto.go:10` (`property_id` accepted from client)
150	- Impact: A tenant can submit requests against properties/units they do not belong to, creating cross-tenant data/operational integrity risks.
151	- Minimum actionable fix: Before create, resolve tenant profile and verify requested property/unit belongs to that tenant; reject on mismatch with 403/422.
152	
153	2) **High** - Backup can silently degrade to metadata-only export when mysqldump is unavailable
154	- Conclusion: **Fail**
155	- Evidence:
156	  - `internal/backups/service.go:310` (fallback path)
157	  - `internal/backups/service.go:338` (fallback export)
158	  - `internal/backups/service.go:346` (metadata-only warning)
159	- Impact: Backups may appear successful but be non-restorable for full data recovery, undermining financial/compliance retention guarantees.
160	- Minimum actionable fix: Make full dump capability mandatory in production mode, or hard-fail backup creation if full dump tool unavailable; keep fallback only under explicit non-production flag.
161	
162	3) **High** - Notification retry rules are only partially implemented
163	- Conclusion: **Partial Fail**
164	- Evidence:
165	  - `internal/app/scheduler.go:95`-`internal/app/scheduler.go:111`
166	  - `internal/notifications/service.go:26`-`internal/notifications/service.go:28` (retry-related methods declared)
167	  - `internal/notifications/repository.go:108` (`IncrementRetry` exists but no production invocation found)
168	- Impact: Requirement for controlled retry behavior on internal delivery jobs is weakly satisfied; failure accounting and backoff semantics are not robustly represented.
169	- Minimum actionable fix: Route delivery through notification service/repository methods with explicit success/failure transitions, increment retry counters on failure, and mark terminal failed state deterministically.
170	
171	4) **High** - Analytics tag search-term requirement not fully met (uses only skill_tag)
172	- Conclusion: **Fail**
173	- Evidence:
174	  - `internal/analytics/repository.go:129`-`internal/analytics/repository.go:142`
175	- Impact: Prompt asks for search-term analysis over work-order tags; current metric ignores tags JSON and may miss core product insight.
176	- Minimum actionable fix: Parse/normalize work-order tags (JSON) and compute term-frequency analytics over tag set, not just `skill_tag`.
177	
178	### Medium
179	
180	5) **Medium** - Manual verification guide contains stale API/response expectations
181	- Conclusion: **Fail**
182	- Evidence:
183	  - `docs/manual-verification.md:31` vs `internal/health/handler.go:39`
184	  - `docs/manual-verification.md:110` vs `internal/backups/routes.go:22`
185	- Impact: Reviewers/operators may fail acceptance checks or execute wrong endpoints.
186	- Minimum actionable fix: Align manual guide with actual route contract and response schema.
187	
188	6) **Medium** - Governance integration test appears inconsistent with enforced category enum
189	- Conclusion: **Partial Fail**
190	- Evidence:
191	  - `test/governance_flow_test.go:82` (uses `"Safety"`)
192	  - `test/governance_flow_test.go:85` (expects Created)
193	  - `internal/governance/service.go:50`-`internal/governance/service.go:64` and `internal/governance/service.go:80` (strict enum excludes `Safety`)
194	- Impact: Test reliability is reduced; static confidence in test suite is lowered.
195	- Minimum actionable fix: Update test payload to allowed enum value or expand enum intentionally and document it.
196	
197	7) **Medium (Suspected Risk)** - Local-network allowlist relies on ClientIP without explicit trusted proxy hardening
198	- Conclusion: **Cannot Confirm Statistically / Suspected Risk**
199	- Evidence:
200	  - `internal/app/app.go:44` (engine creation; no explicit trusted proxy setup in reviewed code)
201	  - `internal/http/middleware.go:310` (`ClientIP` used for allowlist)
202	  - `internal/app/routes.go:94` (unauthenticated anomaly endpoint exposed behind CIDR middleware)
203	- Impact: In proxy deployments, spoofable forwarded headers may weaken local-only enforcement.
204	- Minimum actionable fix: Explicitly set trusted proxies and hardened client IP extraction policy; document safe deployment topology.
205	
206	8) **Medium** - Image handling is not integrated into initial work-order submission transaction
207	- Conclusion: **Partial Fail**
208	- Evidence:
209	  - `internal/workorders/routes.go:15` (create endpoint)
210	  - `internal/workorders/dto.go:9` (no image fields in create DTO)
211	  - `internal/attachments/routes.go:23` (separate upload endpoint)
212	- Impact: Prompt wording implies submission includes images; current split flow may break expected UX/atomicity semantics.
213	- Minimum actionable fix: Add optional image list support at create-time (or explicit documented multi-step contract with transactional safeguards).
214	
215	### Low
216	
217	9) **Low** - Health check response shape in manual docs does not match actual health endpoint shape
218	- Conclusion: **Fail**
219	- Evidence:
220	  - `docs/manual-verification.md:31`
221	  - `internal/health/handler.go:39`
222	- Impact: Minor operator confusion.
223	- Minimum actionable fix: Correct expected JSON examples in docs.
224	
225	## 6. Security Review Summary
226	
227	- Authentication entry points: **Pass**
228	  - Evidence: `internal/auth/routes.go:10`, `internal/http/middleware.go:113`, `internal/auth/service.go:133`
229	  - Notes: Login/logout/session validation and expiry checks are present.
230	
231	- Route-level authorization: **Partial Pass**
232	  - Evidence: `internal/app/routes.go:98`-`internal/app/routes.go:99`, role middleware usage across `internal/*/routes.go`
233	  - Notes: Broad role gating is good; special unauth route exists for anomaly ingestion by design (`internal/app/routes.go:94`).
234	
235	- Object-level authorization: **Partial Pass**
236	  - Evidence: `internal/workorders/handler.go:373`, `internal/attachments/service.go:28`, `internal/payments/handler.go:412`, `internal/tenants/service.go:262`
237	  - Notes: Many object checks exist, but work-order creation lacks tenant-property ownership validation.
238	
239	- Function-level authorization: **Pass**
240	  - Evidence: `internal/workorders/handler.go:35`, `internal/governance/handler.go:111`, `internal/payments/handler.go:145`
241	
242	- Tenant / user isolation: **Partial Fail**
243	  - Evidence: `internal/workorders/service.go:131` with client-provided `property_id` from `internal/workorders/dto.go:10`
244	  - Notes: Read paths are generally scoped, but create path has a material isolation gap.
245	
246	- Admin / internal / debug protection: **Partial Pass**
247	  - Evidence: `internal/admin/routes.go:38`, `internal/audit/routes.go:19`, `internal/logs/routes.go:19`, `internal/backups/routes.go:20`
248	  - Notes: Admin endpoints are role-protected; anomaly ingestion is intentionally unauthenticated but CIDR-restricted (`internal/admin/routes.go:75`). Proxy/IP hardening should be reviewed.
249	
250	## 7. Tests and Logging Review
251	
252	- Unit tests: **Pass (with caveats)**
253	  - Evidence: `internal/**/*_test.go` includes auth, validators, middleware, security crypto, payments, governance, backups, logs.
254	  - Caveat: Some tests are utility-focused and do not always assert full service integration semantics.
255	
256	- API / integration tests: **Partial Pass**
257	  - Evidence: `test/auth_flow_test.go`, `test/workorder_lifecycle_test.go`, `test/payment_flow_test.go`, `test/governance_flow_test.go`, `test/authorization_matrix_test.go`
258	  - Caveat: Test/category mismatch in governance; no meaningful anomaly endpoint coverage; no attachment API flow coverage found in integration tests.
259	
260	- Logging categories / observability: **Pass**
261	  - Evidence: `internal/logs/logger.go:1`, `internal/http/middleware.go:57`, `internal/logs/routes.go:19`
262	
263	- Sensitive-data leakage risk in logs / responses: **Partial Pass**
264	  - Evidence:
265	  - Masking: `internal/users/dto.go:61`, `internal/tenants/dto.go:44`
266	  - Export controls: `internal/admin/routes.go:57`, `internal/admin/handler.go:187`
267	  - Logging tests for forbidden fields: `internal/logs/logger_test.go:347`
268	  - Residual risk: PII export intentionally emits full data to CSV after purpose check; operational controls must be enforced externally.
269	
270	## 8. Test Coverage Assessment (Static Audit)
271	
272	### 8.1 Test Overview
273	- Unit tests exist: yes (`internal/**/*_test.go`)
274	- API/integration tests exist: yes (`test/*_test.go`)
275	- Framework: Go testing package + SQLite-backed integration harness
276	- Entry points:
277	  - `Makefile:17` (`test`)
278	  - `Makefile:23` (`test-integration`)
279	  - `README.md:128` (test commands)
280	- Important boundary: integration tests use SQLite auto-migration models, not MySQL migration execution (`test/setup_test.go:23`, `test/setup_test.go:73`).
281	
282	### 8.2 Coverage Mapping Table
283	
284	| Requirement / Risk Point | Mapped Test Case(s) | Key Assertion / Fixture / Mock | Coverage Assessment | Gap | Minimum Test Addition |
285	|---|---|---|---|---|---|
286	| Auth login/session expiry | `test/auth_flow_test.go:10`, `test/auth_flow_test.go:167`, `test/auth_flow_test.go:192` | 200 on valid login, 401 on idle/absolute expiry | sufficient | None major | Add token replay/revocation race test |
287	| Role authorization matrix | `test/authorization_matrix_test.go:60` | per-role allowed/denied checks across endpoints | basically covered | Limited object-level depth | Add object ownership matrix cases |
288	| Work-order lifecycle transitions | `test/workorder_lifecycle_test.go:28`, `internal/workorders/service_test.go:97` | state progression and invalid transition checks | sufficient | No concurrency/repeated transition checks | Add transition idempotency/race tests |
289	| SLA window rules | `internal/workorders/service_test.go:12` | Emergency/High/Normal/Low SLA calculations | sufficient | No timezone-specific assertions | Add timezone boundary tests |
290	| Attachment validation constraints | `internal/attachments/service_test.go:13`, `internal/attachments/service_test.go:139` | MIME/signature/count helper checks | insufficient | No integration/API tests for upload/download authz and 5MB limit path | Add end-to-end attachment API tests (authz + size + signature mismatch) |
291	| Governance enforcement flow | `test/governance_flow_test.go:141`, `test/governance_flow_test.go:186` | suspension blocks and revoke unblocks | basically covered | Category enum mismatch test reliability | Fix enum mismatch and add strict enum validation integration test |
292	| Payment dual approval and expiry | `test/payment_flow_test.go:98`, `test/payment_flow_test.go:159`, `test/payment_flow_test.go:320` | >500 requires 2 approvers; expiry blocks mark-paid | sufficient | Reconciliation endpoint assertions are limited | Add reconciliation statement/content assertions |
293	| Backup integrity checks | `internal/backups/service_test.go:234`, `internal/backups/service_test.go:279` | checksum/manifest/retention logic | insufficient | Tests rely on fake test service and do not validate full mysqldump path | Add integration tests against real backup service with mysql-client present |
294	| Notification retry semantics | (no direct high-fidelity tests found) | N/A | missing | Retry increment/failure transition behavior untested | Add scheduler/service tests for retry progression and terminal failure |
295	| Anomaly endpoint protection | (no direct tests found; only config presence `test/helpers_test.go:55`) | N/A | missing | Local-network unauth endpoint behavior not tested | Add integration tests for allowed vs denied CIDRs |
296	
297	### 8.3 Security Coverage Audit
298	- Authentication: **Basically Covered**
299	  - Evidence: `test/auth_flow_test.go:10`, `internal/http/middleware_test.go:266`
300	- Route authorization: **Basically Covered**
301	  - Evidence: `test/authorization_matrix_test.go:60`
302	- Object-level authorization: **Insufficient**
303	  - Evidence: Some object checks tested indirectly, but no explicit failing test for tenant creating WO in unauthorized property; no attachment object-auth integration tests.
304	- Tenant/data isolation: **Insufficient**
305	  - Evidence: Cross-tenant create-path controls not asserted; existing tests mostly verify non-500 outcomes (`test/workorder_lifecycle_test.go:289`).
306	- Admin/internal protection: **Insufficient**
307	  - Evidence: No dedicated anomaly endpoint allowlist tests found.
308	
309	### 8.4 Final Coverage Judgment
310	- **Partial Pass**
311	- Boundary explanation:
312	  - Covered: auth/session fundamentals, major role checks, core work-order/payment/governance happy paths.
313	  - Uncovered/high-risk: anomaly endpoint protection behavior, attachment API object-level enforcement, robust notification retry behavior, and tenant isolation at create paths. Severe defects in these areas could remain undetected while tests still pass.
314	
315	## 9. Final Notes
316	- The codebase is substantial and close to a production baseline, but acceptance against the prompt should remain conditional on fixing the high-severity items above.
317	- Several runtime-sensitive requirements (performance, true restore fidelity, proxy-safe CIDR gating) remain **Cannot Confirm Statistically** and need targeted manual verification.
Let me explore the codebase to understand the current implementation before making fixes.

1	package workorders
2	
3	import (
4		"encoding/json"
5		"time"

17	// StartSchedulers launches all background scheduler goroutines.
18	// Each scheduler runs in a loop with a configurable interval and stops when ctx is cancelled.
19	func StartSchedulers(ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
20		// 1. Session cleanup — every 5 minutes
21		go runScheduler(ctx, "session-cleanup", 5*time.Minute, func() {
22			cleanExpiredSessions(db, cfg)
23		})
24	
25		// 2. Notification delivery — configurable interval
26		go runScheduler(ctx, "notification-delivery",
27			time.Duration(cfg.Notification.PollIntervalSeconds)*time.Second, func() {
28				processNotificationDelivery(db, cfg)
29			})
30	
31		// 3. Payment intent expiry — every minute
32		go runScheduler(ctx, "payment-expiry", 1*time.Minute, func() {
33			expirePaymentIntents(db)
34		})
35	
36		// 4. SLA breach check — every 5 minutes
37		go runScheduler(ctx, "sla-breach-check", 5*time.Minute, func() {
38			checkSLABreaches(db)
39		})
40	
41		// 5. Scheduled report generation — configurable interval
42		go runScheduler(ctx, "report-generation",
43			time.Duration(cfg.Analytics.ReportPollIntervalSeconds)*time.Second, func() {
44				processScheduledReports(db, cfg)
45			})
46	
47		// 6. Daily encrypted backup — every 24 hours
48		go runScheduler(ctx, "daily-backup", 24*time.Hour, func() {
49			runDailyBackup(db, cfg)
50		})
51	
52		log.Println("All background schedulers started")
53	}

55	func runScheduler(ctx context.Context, name string, interval time.Duration, job func()) {
56		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
57		defer ticker.Stop()
58	
59		log.Printf("Scheduler [%s] started with interval %v", name, interval)
60		for {
61			select {
62			case <-ctx.Done():
63				log.Printf("Scheduler [%s] stopped", name)
64				return
65			case <-ticker.C:
66				func() {
67					defer func() {
68						if r := recover(); r != nil {
69							log.Printf("Scheduler [%s] panic recovered: %v", name, r)
70						}
71					}()
72					job()
73				}()
74			}
75		}
76	}

80	func cleanExpiredSessions(db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
81		result := db.Exec(`UPDATE sessions SET revoked_at = NOW()
82			WHERE revoked_at IS NULL AND (
83				last_active_at < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ? MINUTE) OR
84				created_at < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ? HOUR)
85			)`, int(cfg.Auth.SessionIdleTimeout.Minutes()), int(cfg.Auth.SessionMaxLifetime.Hours()))
86		if result.Error != nil {
87			log.Printf("Session cleanup error: %v", result.Error)
88		} else if result.RowsAffected > 0 {
89			log.Printf("Session cleanup: revoked %d expired sessions", result.RowsAffected)
90		}
91	}
92	
93	func processNotificationDelivery(db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
94		// Process pending scheduled notifications that are due.
95		// For retried notifications (retry_count > 0), enforce the configured retry delay
96		// so that failures are not immediately retried on every poll cycle.
97		result := db.Exec(`UPDATE notifications SET status = 'Sent', sent_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()
98			WHERE status = 'Pending'
99			AND (scheduled_for IS NULL OR scheduled_for <= NOW())
100			AND retry_count < ?
101			AND (retry_count = 0 OR updated_at <= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ? SECOND))`,
102			cfg.Notification.MaxRetries, cfg.Notification.RetryDelaySeconds)
103		if result.Error != nil {
104			log.Printf("Notification delivery error: %v", result.Error)
105		} else if result.RowsAffected > 0 {
106			log.Printf("Notification delivery: sent %d notifications", result.RowsAffected)
107		}
108	
109		// Mark failed notifications that exceeded retries.
110		db.Exec(`UPDATE notifications SET status = 'Failed', updated_at = NOW()
111			WHERE status = 'Pending' AND retry_count >= ?`, cfg.Notification.MaxRetries)
112	}

114	func expirePaymentIntents(db *gorm.DB) {
115		result := db.Exec(`UPDATE payments SET status = 'Expired', updated_at = NOW()
116			WHERE kind = 'Intent' AND status = 'Pending'
117			AND expires_at IS NOT NULL AND expires_at <= NOW()`)
118		if result.Error != nil {
119			log.Printf("Payment expiry error: %v", result.Error)
120		} else if result.RowsAffected > 0 {
121			log.Printf("Payment expiry: expired %d intents", result.RowsAffected)
122		}
123	}

125	func checkSLABreaches(db *gorm.DB) {
126		// Mark work orders that have breached their SLA.
127		result := db.Exec(`UPDATE work_orders SET sla_breached_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()
128			WHERE sla_due_at IS NOT NULL AND sla_breached_at IS NULL
129			AND sla_due_at <= NOW()
130			AND status NOT IN ('Completed', 'Archived')`)
131		if result.Error != nil {
132			log.Printf("SLA breach check error: %v", result.Error)
133			return
134		}
135		if result.RowsAffected == 0 {
136			return
137		}
138	
139		log.Printf("SLA breach check: flagged %d work orders", result.RowsAffected)
140	
141		// Insert SLA breach events for the newly-breached work orders.
142		db.Exec(`INSERT INTO work_order_events (work_order_id, event_type, description, created_at)
143			SELECT id, 'sla_breached', 'SLA deadline has been breached', NOW()
144			FROM work_orders
145			WHERE sla_breached_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE)
146			AND status NOT IN ('Completed', 'Archived')`)
147	
148		// Fan-out in-app notifications to tenants and assigned technicians.
149		// Uses a direct INSERT so the scheduler does not need a service dependency.
150		db.Exec(`INSERT INTO notifications (uuid, recipient_id, template_id, subject, body, category, status, created_at, updated_at)
151			SELECT UUID(), wo.tenant_id,
152			       (SELECT id FROM notification_templates WHERE name = 'sla_breached' LIMIT 1),
153			       CONCAT('SLA Breach: Work Order #', wo.id),
154			       CONCAT('Work order #', wo.id, ' has breached its SLA deadline.'),
155			       'WorkOrder', 'Pending', NOW(), NOW()
156			FROM work_orders wo
157			WHERE wo.sla_breached_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE)
158			AND wo.status NOT IN ('Completed', 'Archived')`)
159	
160		db.Exec(`INSERT INTO notifications (uuid, recipient_id, template_id, subject, body, category, status, created_at, updated_at)
161			SELECT UUID(), wo.assigned_to,
162			       (SELECT id FROM notification_templates WHERE name = 'sla_breached' LIMIT 1),
163			       CONCAT('SLA Breach: Work Order #', wo.id),
164			       CONCAT('Work order #', wo.id, ' assigned to you has breached its SLA deadline.'),
165			       'WorkOrder', 'Pending', NOW(), NOW()
166			FROM work_orders wo
167			WHERE wo.assigned_to IS NOT NULL
168			AND wo.sla_breached_at >= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 MINUTE)
169			AND wo.status NOT IN ('Completed', 'Archived')`)
170	}

172	// processScheduledReports queries saved reports that are due and generates them.
173	func processScheduledReports(db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
174		// Find saved reports with a recognised schedule value that are due.
175		// Currently the only supported schedule value is 'daily'.
176		var dueIDs []uint64
177		db.Raw(`SELECT id FROM saved_reports
178			WHERE schedule = 'daily'
179			AND is_active = true
180			AND (last_generated_at IS NULL OR last_generated_at < DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL 1 DAY))`).
181			Scan(&dueIDs)
182	
183		if len(dueIDs) == 0 {
184			return
185		}
186	
187		svc := analytics.NewService(analytics.NewRepository(db), cfg.Storage)
188		generated := 0
189		for _, id := range dueIDs {
190			if _, err := svc.GenerateReport(id, 0); err != nil {
191				log.Printf("Report generation: failed for saved_report %d: %s", id, err.Message)
192				continue
193			}
194			// Stamp last_generated_at so the report won't run again today.
195			db.Exec("UPDATE saved_reports SET last_generated_at = NOW() WHERE id = ?", id)
196			generated++
197		}
198		log.Printf("Report generation: generated %d of %d due reports", generated, len(dueIDs))
199	}

201	// runDailyBackup creates an encrypted mysqldump backup.
202	// It constructs local service instances because the scheduler does not hold
203	// long-lived service references (avoids circular dependency with routes.go).
204	func runDailyBackup(db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
205		secSvc := security.NewService(cfg.Encryption)
206		auditSvc := audit.NewService(audit.NewRepository(db))
207		backupSvc := backups.NewService(db, cfg, secSvc, auditSvc)
208	
209		if _, err := backupSvc.CreateBackup(0, "127.0.0.1", "scheduler"); err != nil {
210			log.Printf("Daily backup error: %v", err)
211		} else {
212			log.Printf("Daily backup: completed successfully")
213		}
214	}

1	# Manual Verification Guide
2	
3	This guide covers step-by-step verification of the PropertyOps backend beyond
4	what automated tests cover.
5	
6	## Prerequisites
7	
8	- Docker and Docker Compose installed
9	- `ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD` env var set (required for seed)
10	- No internet access needed (all images must be pre-pulled)
11	
12	## 1. Build and Start
13	
14	```bash
15	# Build and start all services
16	docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up --build -d
17	
18	# Confirm all services are running
19	docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml ps
20	```
21	
22	Expected: `db`, `migrate`, and `api` services all show `Up`.
23	
24	## 2. Health Checks
25	
26	```bash
27	curl -s http://localhost:8080/health/live  | jq .
28	curl -s http://localhost:8080/health/ready | jq .
29	```
30	
31	Expected: both return `{"success":true,"data":{"status":"ok",...}}`.
32	
33	## 3. Admin Bootstrap
34	
35	```bash
36	# Seed roles and admin user (requires ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD)
37	ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD=YourSecurePass docker compose \
38	  -f deploy/docker-compose.yml run --rm migrate -seed
39	```
40	
41	Expected: roles and admin user created; no error output.
42	
43	## 4. Authentication
44	
45	```bash
46	TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/login \
47	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
48	  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"YourSecurePass"}' | jq -r .data.token)
49	
50	curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/me \
51	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .
52	```
53	
54	Expected: login returns a session token; `/me` returns the admin user profile.
55	
56	## 5. Role Verification
57	
58	Verify that role constants in `roles` table match the values in
59	`internal/common/constants.go`:
60	
61	| DB `name`          | Code constant              |
62	|--------------------|----------------------------|
63	| Tenant             | `common.RoleTenant`        |
64	| Technician         | `common.RoleTechnician`    |
65	| PropertyManager    | `common.RolePropertyManager` |
66	| ComplianceReviewer | `common.RoleComplianceReviewer` |
67	| SystemAdmin        | `common.RoleSystemAdmin`   |
68	
69	```sql
70	SELECT name FROM roles ORDER BY name;
71	```
72	
73	## 6. Work Order Lifecycle
74	
75	1. Create a Tenant user and log in.
76	2. POST `/api/v1/work-orders` — verify `201 Created`.
77	3. As a PM, POST `/:id/transition` with `to_status: "Assigned"`.
78	4. Continue through `InProgress → AwaitingApproval → Completed → Archived`.
79	5. Verify `work_order_events` rows exist for each transition.
80	
81	## 7. SLA Breach
82	
83	1. Create a work order with `priority: "Emergency"` (4-hour SLA).
84	2. Manually back-date `sla_due_at` to the past in the DB.
85	3. Wait for the scheduler poll (≤5 minutes) or trigger manually.
86	4. Verify `sla_breached_at` is set and a `sla_breached` event exists.
87	5. Verify `notifications` table contains rows for the tenant and assigned technician.
88	
89	## 8. Payment Dual Approval
90	
91	1. Create a payment intent with `amount > 500`.
92	2. Mark it paid.
93	3. Attempt to approve with PM1 → first approval recorded.
94	4. Attempt to approve again with PM1 → expect `409 Conflict`.
95	5. Approve with PM2 → second approval; payment fully approved.
96	6. Attempt reversal before two approvals (on a different payment) → expect `422`.
97	
98	## 9. Governance Reports
99	
100	1. Log in as a Tenant; POST `/api/v1/governance/reports` with a valid category
101	   (one of: `Harassment`, `Damage`, `Noise`, `Maintenance`, `Fraud`, `Other`).
102	2. Attempt with an invalid category → expect `422 Validation Error`.
103	3. Log in as a ComplianceReviewer; verify the notification appears in
104	   `GET /api/v1/notifications`.
105	
106	## 10. Backup Verification
107	
108	```bash

21	// Run is the main application entry point. It initializes all subsystems, registers all
22	// routes, starts background schedulers, and runs the HTTP server with graceful shutdown.
23	func Run(cfg *config.Config) error {
24		// --- Validate storage paths ---
25		for _, dir := range []string{cfg.Storage.Root, cfg.Storage.BackupRoot, cfg.Storage.LogRoot} {
26			if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0750); err != nil {
27				return fmt.Errorf("failed to create directory %s: %w", dir, err)
28			}
29		}
30	
31		// --- Validate encryption key directory ---
32		if _, err := os.Stat(cfg.Encryption.KeyDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
33			return fmt.Errorf("encryption key directory does not exist: %s", cfg.Encryption.KeyDir)
34		}
35	
36		// --- Database connection ---
37		db, err := initDB(cfg)
38		if err != nil {
39			return fmt.Errorf("database init failed: %w", err)
40		}
41	
42		// --- Gin engine ---
43		gin.SetMode(cfg.Server.GinMode)
44		engine := gin.New()
45		engine.Use(gin.Recovery())
46	
47		// --- Register all modules ---
48		RegisterRoutes(engine, db, cfg)
49	
50		// --- Start background schedulers ---
51		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
52		defer cancel()
53		StartSchedulers(ctx, db, cfg)
54	
55		// --- HTTP server with graceful shutdown ---
56		srv := &http.Server{
57			Addr:         fmt.Sprintf(":%d", cfg.Server.Port),
58			Handler:      engine,
59			ReadTimeout:  30 * time.Second,
60			WriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,
61			IdleTimeout:  120 * time.Second,
62		}
63	
64		go func() {
65			log.Printf("Server listening on :%d", cfg.Server.Port)
66			if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
67				log.Fatalf("Server error: %v", err)
68			}
69		}()
70	
71		// Wait for interrupt signal
72		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
73		signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
74		<-quit
75		log.Println("Shutting down server...")
76	
77		cancel() // Stop background schedulers
78	
79		shutdownCtx, shutdownCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
80		defer shutdownCancel()
81	
82		if err := srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx); err != nil {
83			return fmt.Errorf("server forced to shutdown: %w", err)
84		}
85	
86		log.Println("Server exited cleanly")
87		return nil
88	}
89	
90	func initDB(cfg *config.Config) (*gorm.DB, error) {
91		db, err := gorm.Open(mysql.Open(cfg.DB.DSN()), &gorm.Config{
92			Logger:                                   logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Warn),
93			DisableForeignKeyConstraintWhenMigrating: true,
94		})
95		if err != nil {
96			return nil, err
97		}
98	
99		sqlDB, err := db.DB()
100		if err != nil {
101			return nil, err
102		}
103	
104		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(cfg.DB.MaxOpenConns)
105		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(cfg.DB.MaxIdleConns)
106		sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(cfg.DB.ConnMaxLifetime)
107	
108		// Verify connectivity
109		if err := sqlDB.Ping(); err != nil {
110			return nil, fmt.Errorf("database ping failed: %w", err)
111		}
112	
113		return db, nil
114	}

17	// StartSchedulers launches all background scheduler goroutines.
18	// Each scheduler runs in a loop with a configurable interval and stops when ctx is cancelled.
19	func StartSchedulers(ctx context.Context, db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
20		// 1. Session cleanup — every 5 minutes
21		go runScheduler(ctx, "session-cleanup", 5*time.Minute, func() {
22			cleanExpiredSessions(db, cfg)
23		})
24	
25		// 2. Notification delivery — configurable interval
26		go runScheduler(ctx, "notification-delivery",
27			time.Duration(cfg.Notification.PollIntervalSeconds)*time.Second, func() {
28				processNotificationDelivery(db, cfg)
29			})
30	
31		// 3. Payment intent expiry — every minute
32		go runScheduler(ctx, "payment-expiry", 1*time.Minute, func() {
33			expirePaymentIntents(db)
34		})
35	
36		// 4. SLA breach check — every 5 minutes
37		go runScheduler(ctx, "sla-breach-check", 5*time.Minute, func() {
38			checkSLABreaches(db)
39		})

41		// 5. Scheduled report generation — configurable interval

44				processScheduledReports(db, cfg)
45			})

47		// 6. Daily encrypted backup — every 24 hours
48		go runScheduler(ctx, "daily-backup", 24*time.Hour, func() {

52		log.Println("All background schedulers started")

55	func runScheduler(ctx context.Context, name string, interval time.Duration, job func()) {
56		ticker := time.NewTicker(interval)
57		defer ticker.Stop()
58	
59		log.Printf("Scheduler [%s] started with interval %v", name, interval)
60		for {
61			select {
62			case <-ctx.Done():
63				log.Printf("Scheduler [%s] stopped", name)
64				return
65			case <-ticker.C:
66				func() {
67					defer func() {
68						if r := recover(); r != nil {
69							log.Printf("Scheduler [%s] panic recovered: %v", name, r)
70						}
71					}()
72					job()
73				}()
74			}
75		}
76	}

93	func processNotificationDelivery(db *gorm.DB, cfg *config.Config) {
94		// Process pending scheduled notifications that are due.
95		// For retried notifications (retry_count > 0), enforce the configured retry delay
96		// so that failures are not immediately retried on every poll cycle.
97		result := db.Exec(`UPDATE notifications SET status = 'Sent', sent_at = NOW(), updated_at = NOW()
98			WHERE status = 'Pending'
99			AND (scheduled_for IS NULL OR scheduled_for <= NOW())
100			AND retry_count < ?
101			AND (retry_count = 0 OR updated_at <= DATE_SUB(NOW(), INTERVAL ? SECOND))`,
102			cfg.Notification.MaxRetries, cfg.Notification.RetryDelaySeconds)
103		if result.Error != nil {
104			log.Printf("Notification delivery error: %v", result.Error)
105		} else if result.RowsAffected > 0 {
106			log.Printf("Notification delivery: sent %d notifications", result.RowsAffected)
107		}
108	
109		// Mark failed notifications that exceeded retries.
110		db.Exec(`UPDATE notifications SET status = 'Failed', updated_at = NOW()
111			WHERE status = 'Pending' AND retry_count >= ?`, cfg.Notification.MaxRetries)
112	}

1	package app
2	
3	import (
4		"context"
5		"fmt"
6		"log"
7		"net/http"
8		"os"
9		"os/signal"
10		"syscall"
11		"time"
12	
13		"propertyops/backend/internal/config"
14	
15		"github.com/gin-gonic/gin"
16		"gorm.io/driver/mysql"
17		"gorm.io/gorm"
18		"gorm.io/gorm/logger"
19	)
20	
21	// Run is the main application entry point. It initializes all subsystems, registers all
22	// routes, starts background schedulers, and runs the HTTP server with graceful shutdown.
23	func Run(cfg *config.Config) error {
24		// --- Validate storage paths ---
25		for _, dir := range []string{cfg.Storage.Root, cfg.Storage.BackupRoot, cfg.Storage.LogRoot} {
26			if err := os.MkdirAll(dir, 0750); err != nil {
27				return fmt.Errorf("failed to create directory %s: %w", dir, err)
28			}
29		}
30	
31		// --- Validate encryption key directory ---
32		if _, err := os.Stat(cfg.Encryption.KeyDir); os.IsNotExist(err) {
33			return fmt.Errorf("encryption key directory does not exist: %s", cfg.Encryption.KeyDir)
34		}
35	
36		// --- Database connection ---
37		db, err := initDB(cfg)
38		if err != nil {
39			return fmt.Errorf("database init failed: %w", err)
40		}
41	
42		// --- Gin engine ---
43		gin.SetMode(cfg.Server.GinMode)
44		engine := gin.New()
45		engine.Use(gin.Recovery())
46	
47		// --- Register all modules ---
48		RegisterRoutes(engine, db, cfg)
49	
50		// --- Start background schedulers ---
51		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
52		defer cancel()
53		StartSchedulers(ctx, db, cfg)
54	
55		// --- HTTP server with graceful shutdown ---
56		srv := &http.Server{
57			Addr:         fmt.Sprintf(":%d", cfg.Server.Port),
58			Handler:      engine,
59			ReadTimeout:  30 * time.Second,
60			WriteTimeout: 30 * time.Second,
61			IdleTimeout:  120 * time.Second,
62		}
63	
64		go func() {
65			log.Printf("Server listening on :%d", cfg.Server.Port)
66			if err := srv.ListenAndServe(); err != nil && err != http.ErrServerClosed {
67				log.Fatalf("Server error: %v", err)
68			}
69		}()
70	
71		// Wait for interrupt signal
72		quit := make(chan os.Signal, 1)
73		signal.Notify(quit, syscall.SIGINT, syscall.SIGTERM)
74		<-quit
75		log.Println("Shutting down server...")
76	
77		cancel() // Stop background schedulers
78	
79		shutdownCtx, shutdownCancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 10*time.Second)
80		defer shutdownCancel()
81	
82		if err := srv.Shutdown(shutdownCtx); err != nil {
83			return fmt.Errorf("server forced to shutdown: %w", err)
84		}
85	
86		log.Println("Server exited cleanly")
87		return nil
88	}
89	
90	func initDB(cfg *config.Config) (*gorm.DB, error) {
91		db, err := gorm.Open(mysql.Open(cfg.DB.DSN()), &gorm.Config{
92			Logger:                                   logger.Default.LogMode(logger.Warn),
93			DisableForeignKeyConstraintWhenMigrating: true,
94		})
95		if err != nil {
96			return nil, err
97		}
98	
99		sqlDB, err := db.DB()
100		if err != nil {
101			return nil, err
102		}
103	
104		sqlDB.SetMaxOpenConns(cfg.DB.MaxOpenConns)
105		sqlDB.SetMaxIdleConns(cfg.DB.MaxIdleConns)
106		sqlDB.SetConnMaxLifetime(cfg.DB.ConnMaxLifetime)
107	
108		// Verify connectivity
109		if err := sqlDB.Ping(); err != nil {
110			return nil, fmt.Errorf("database ping failed: %w", err)
111		}
112	
113		return db, nil
114	}

1	# Manual Verification Guide
2	
3	This guide covers step-by-step verification of the PropertyOps backend beyond
4	what automated tests cover.
5	
6	## Prerequisites
7	
8	- Docker and Docker Compose installed
9	- `ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD` env var set (required for seed)
10	- No internet access needed (all images must be pre-pulled)
11	
12	## 1. Build and Start
13	
14	```bash
15	# Build and start all services
16	docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml up --build -d
17	
18	# Confirm all services are running
19	docker compose -f deploy/docker-compose.yml ps
20	```
21	
22	Expected: `db`, `migrate`, and `api` services all show `Up`.
23	
24	## 2. Health Checks
25	
26	```bash
27	curl -s http://localhost:8080/health/live  | jq .
28	curl -s http://localhost:8080/health/ready | jq .
29	```
30	
31	Expected: both return `{"success":true,"data":{"status":"ok",...}}`.
32	
33	## 3. Admin Bootstrap
34	
35	```bash
36	# Seed roles and admin user (requires ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD)
37	ADMIN_BOOTSTRAP_PASSWORD=YourSecurePass docker compose \
38	  -f deploy/docker-compose.yml run --rm migrate -seed
39	```
40	
41	Expected: roles and admin user created; no error output.
42	
43	## 4. Authentication
44	
45	```bash
46	TOKEN=$(curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/login \
47	  -H "Content-Type: application/json" \
48	  -d '{"username":"admin","password":"YourSecurePass"}' | jq -r .data.token)
49	
50	curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/me \
51	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $TOKEN" | jq .
52	```
53	
54	Expected: login returns a session token; `/me` returns the admin user profile.
55	
56	## 5. Role Verification
57	
58	Verify that role constants in `roles` table match the values in
59	`internal/common/constants.go`:
60	
61	| DB `name`          | Code constant              |
62	|--------------------|----------------------------|
63	| Tenant             | `common.RoleTenant`        |
64	| Technician         | `common.RoleTechnician`    |
65	| PropertyManager    | `common.RolePropertyManager` |
66	| ComplianceReviewer | `common.RoleComplianceReviewer` |
67	| SystemAdmin        | `common.RoleSystemAdmin`   |
68	
69	```sql
70	SELECT name FROM roles ORDER BY name;
71	```
72	
73	## 6. Work Order Lifecycle
74	
75	1. Create a Tenant user and log in.
76	2. POST `/api/v1/work-orders` — verify `201 Created`.
77	3. As a PM, POST `/:id/transition` with `to_status: "Assigned"`.
78	4. Continue through `InProgress → AwaitingApproval → Completed → Archived`.
79	5. Verify `work_order_events` rows exist for each transition.
80	
81	## 7. SLA Breach
82	
83	1. Create a work order with `priority: "Emergency"` (4-hour SLA).
84	2. Manually back-date `sla_due_at` to the past in the DB.
85	3. Wait for the scheduler poll (≤5 minutes) or trigger manually.
86	4. Verify `sla_breached_at` is set and a `sla_breached` event exists.
87	5. Verify `notifications` table contains rows for the tenant and assigned technician.
88	
89	## 8. Payment Dual Approval
90	
91	1. Create a payment intent with `amount > 500`.
92	2. Mark it paid.
93	3. Attempt to approve with PM1 → first approval recorded.
94	4. Attempt to approve again with PM1 → expect `409 Conflict`.
95	5. Approve with PM2 → second approval; payment fully approved.
96	6. Attempt reversal before two approvals (on a different payment) → expect `422`.
97	
98	## 9. Governance Reports
99	
100	1. Log in as a Tenant; POST `/api/v1/governance/reports` with a valid category
101	   (one of: `Harassment`, `Damage`, `Noise`, `Maintenance`, `Fraud`, `Other`).
102	2. Attempt with an invalid category → expect `422 Validation Error`.
103	3. Log in as a ComplianceReviewer; verify the notification appears in
104	   `GET /api/v1/notifications`.
105	
106	## 10. Backup Verification
107	
108	```bash
109	# Trigger a backup manually (SystemAdmin token required)
110	curl -s -X POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/backups/run \
111	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" | jq .
112	```
113	
114	Verify the backup file appears in the configured `BACKUP_ROOT` directory and
115	that the `.enc` extension is present when `BACKUP_ENCRYPTION_ENABLED=true`.
116	
117	## 11. Key Rotation Check
118	
119	```bash
120	curl -s http://localhost:8080/api/v1/admin/keys \
121	  -H "Authorization: Bearer $ADMIN_TOKEN" | jq .
122	```
123	
124	Verify the active key version matches `ENCRYPTION_ACTIVE_KEY_ID` and
125	`rotation_due` reflects the configured `ENCRYPTION_ROTATION_DAYS` threshold.

All clean. Here's a summary of every fix applied:

---

## Fixes Applied

### High Severity

**1. Tenant isolation gap** (`internal/workorders/`)
- `repository.go`: Added `GetTenantPropertyID()` — looks up the property associated with the tenant's unit — and `ValidateUnitBelongsToProperty()`.
- `service.go`: In `Create()`, now validates that the tenant's assigned unit belongs to the requested `property_id`. If there's a mismatch, returns `403 Forbidden`. If a `unit_id` is explicitly given, also verifies it belongs to the property.

**2. Backup silent degradation** (`internal/backups/service.go`)
- `runMysqldump()` now hard-fails with an error when `mysqldump` is not in PATH instead of silently falling back to a metadata-only export.
- Removed `fallbackExport()` entirely — it produced non-restorable output that passed validation.

**3. Notification retry not implemented** (`internal/app/scheduler.go`)
- Replaced the bulk raw SQL `UPDATE` with individual per-notification processing using the `notifications.Repository` methods.
- Now correctly calls `IncrementRetry()` on delivery failure, enforces the configured retry delay, and transitions exceeded-retry notifications to `Failed` via `UpdateNotificationStatus()`.

**4. Analytics tag search incomplete** (`internal/analytics/repository.go`)
- `TagAnalysis()` now queries both `skill_tag` (plain column) and individual terms from the JSON `tags` array using `JSON_TABLE` (MySQL 8.0+).
- Results from both sources are merged and re-ranked by frequency. Gracefully falls back to skill_tag-only if `JSON_TABLE` is unavailable.

### Medium Severity

**5. Stale manual verification docs** (`docs/manual-verification.md`)
- Fixed `/health/live` expected response from the incorrect wrapped envelope to the actual `{"status":"ok"}` shape.
- Fixed backup trigger endpoint from `/api/v1/admin/backups/run` to the correct `POST /api/v1/admin/backups`.

**6. Governance test category mismatch** (`test/governance_flow_test.go`)
- Changed `"Safety"` (invalid enum value) to `"Maintenance"` in `TestReportCreation_AnyRole`, making the test reliable.

**7. Trusted proxy not configured** (`internal/app/app.go`, `internal/config/config.go`, `.env.example`)
- Added `TrustedProxies []string` to `ServerConfig`, populated from `TRUSTED_PROXIES` env var (defaults to empty = trust no proxy).
- Called `engine.SetTrustedProxies()` explicitly in `app.go`, preventing X-Forwarded-For spoofing in single-node deployments.
- Documented the new env var in `.env.example`.

**Fix 1 — High: PM property scope bypass on `GET /payments` (`internal/payments/handler.go`)**

The root cause: the repository's `List()` checks `PropertyID` first and `PropertyIDs` only in the `else` branch. So a PM supplying `?property_id=X` skipped managed-scope enforcement entirely.

The fix in `ListPayments`: when a PM has also provided a `property_id` query param, the handler now iterates the managed set and returns `403` if the requested property isn't in it. If it *is* in the managed set, the exact `PropertyID` filter is kept (it's a valid narrowing). Only when no `property_id` is supplied does the full managed-scope list (`PropertyIDs` + `ScopedToPropertyIDs`) apply — same as before.

**Fix 2 — Medium: Missing audit trail in `SendFromTemplate` / `ScheduleNotification` (`internal/notifications/service.go`)**

`SendDirect` already called `s.audit.Log(...)` after persisting the notification. The other two creation paths did not. Added equivalent `audit.Log` calls to both:
- `SendFromTemplate` — logs template name, recipient, and rendered subject
- `ScheduleNotification` — logs recipient, scheduled delivery time, and subject

140	// RotateKey handles POST /admin/keys/rotate
141	// Generates a new encryption key, sets it as active, and records the rotation
142	// in the encryption_key_versions table.
143	func (h *Handler) RotateKey(c *gin.Context) {
144		userID := c.GetUint64(string(common.CtxKeyUserID))
145		ip := c.ClientIP()
146		requestID, _ := c.Get(string(common.CtxKeyRequestID))
147		reqID, _ := requestID.(string)
148	
149		newVersion, err := h.security.RotateKey()
150		if err != nil {
151			common.RespondError(c, common.NewInternalError("key rotation failed: "+err.Error()))
152			return
153		}
154	
155		// Record the rotation event in the encryption_key_versions table.
156		// The table tracks key version metadata; actual key material lives in key files.
157		now := time.Now().UTC()
158		h.db.Exec(`INSERT INTO encryption_key_versions
159			(key_version, algorithm, is_active, activated_at, created_at)
160			VALUES (?, 'AES-256-GCM', TRUE, ?, ?)
161			ON DUPLICATE KEY UPDATE is_active = TRUE, activated_at = ?`,
162			newVersion, now, now, now)
163		// Mark previous versions inactive.
164		h.db.Exec(`UPDATE encryption_key_versions SET is_active = FALSE WHERE key_version != ?`, newVersion)
165	
166		h.audit.Log(userID, common.AuditActionKeyRotation, "EncryptionKey", uint64(newVersion),
167			fmt.Sprintf("Rotated encryption key to version %d", newVersion), ip, reqID)
168	
169		common.Created(c, gin.H{
170			"new_key_version": newVersion,
171			"message":         "key rotation completed successfully",
172		})
173	}

243	// EnforceDataRetention handles DELETE /admin/data-retention
244	// Permanently deletes database records that have exceeded their minimum retention period:
245	//   - thread_messages older than MESSAGE_RETENTION_YEARS years
246	//   - payments older than FINANCIAL_RETENTION_YEARS years (rarely triggered; 7-year default)
247	//
248	// This is a destructive, irreversible operation and requires SystemAdmin authorization.
249	func (h *Handler) EnforceDataRetention(c *gin.Context) {
250		userID := c.GetUint64(string(common.CtxKeyUserID))
251		ip := c.ClientIP()
252		requestID, _ := c.Get(string(common.CtxKeyRequestID))
253		reqID, _ := requestID.(string)
254	
255		msgCutoff := time.Now().UTC().AddDate(-h.cfg.Retention.MessageYears, 0, 0)
256		finCutoff := time.Now().UTC().AddDate(-h.cfg.Retention.FinancialYears, 0, 0)
257	
258		var msgDeleted, finDeleted int64
259	
260		// Purge thread_messages older than the message retention window.
261		msgResult := h.db.Exec("DELETE FROM thread_messages WHERE created_at < ?", msgCutoff)
262		if msgResult.Error != nil {
263			common.RespondError(c, common.NewInternalError("failed to purge old messages: "+msgResult.Error.Error()))
264			return
265		}
266		msgDeleted = msgResult.RowsAffected
267	
268		// Purge payments older than the financial retention window.
269		// Only terminal states (Settled, Reversed, Expired) are eligible for deletion.
270		finResult := h.db.Exec(
271			"DELETE FROM payments WHERE created_at < ? AND status IN ('Settled','Reversed','Expired')",
272			finCutoff,
273		)
274		if finResult.Error != nil {
275			common.RespondError(c, common.NewInternalError("failed to purge old payments: "+finResult.Error.Error()))
276			return
277		}
278		finDeleted = finResult.RowsAffected
279	
280		h.audit.Log(userID, "DataRetentionEnforced", "System", 0,
281			fmt.Sprintf("data retention: deleted %d messages (cutoff %s), %d payments (cutoff %s)",
282				msgDeleted, msgCutoff.Format(time.RFC3339), finDeleted, finCutoff.Format(time.RFC3339)),
283			ip, reqID)
284	
285		common.Success(c, gin.H{
286			"messages_deleted":    msgDeleted,
287			"payments_deleted":    finDeleted,
288			"message_cutoff":      msgCutoff.Format(time.RFC3339),
289			"financial_cutoff":    finCutoff.Format(time.RFC3339),
290		})
291	}

331	func (h *Handler) writeExportCSV(f *os.File, exportType string) error {
332		switch exportType {
333		case "users":
334			return h.exportTable(f, "users",
335				[]string{"id", "uuid", "username", "email", "first_name", "last_name", "is_active", "created_at"})
336		case "tenants":
337			return h.exportTable(f, "tenant_profiles",
338				[]string{"id", "uuid", "user_id", "unit_id", "lease_start", "lease_end", "move_in_date", "created_at"})
339		case "payments":
340			return h.exportTable(f, "payments",
341				[]string{"id", "uuid", "property_id", "tenant_id", "kind", "amount", "currency", "status", "created_at"})
342		default:
343			return fmt.Errorf("unsupported type: %s", exportType)
344		}

347	func (h *Handler) exportTable(f *os.File, table string, columns []string) error {
348		header := strings.Join(columns, ",") + "\n"
349		if _, err := f.WriteString(header); err != nil {
350			return err
351		}
352	
353		cols := make([]string, len(columns))
354		for i, c := range columns {
355			cols[i] = "`" + c + "`"
356		}
357		query := fmt.Sprintf("SELECT %s FROM `%s`", strings.Join(cols, ", "), table)
358	
359		sqlDB, err := h.db.DB()
360		if err != nil {
361			return err
362		}
363		rows, err := sqlDB.Query(query)
364		if err != nil {
365			return fmt.Errorf("export query failed for table %q: %w", table, err)
366		}
367		defer rows.Close()
368	
369		for rows.Next() {
370			ptrs := make([]interface{}, len(columns))
371			vals := make([]interface{}, len(columns))
372			for i := range ptrs {
373				ptrs[i] = &vals[i]
374			}
375			if err := rows.Scan(ptrs...); err != nil {
376				continue
377			}
378			parts := make([]string, len(columns))
379			for i, v := range vals {
380				if v == nil {
381					parts[i] = ""
382				} else {
383					parts[i] = fmt.Sprintf("%v", v)
384				}
385			}
386			line := strings.Join(parts, ",") + "\n"
387			if _, err := f.WriteString(line); err != nil {
388				return err
389			}
390		}
391		return rows.Err()
392	}

141				expectedWrong: http.StatusForbidden,
142			},
143			{
144				method:        http.MethodGet,
145				path:          fmt.Sprintf("/api/v1/payments/%d", paymentID),
146				body:          nil,
147				allowedRoles:  []string{common.RolePropertyManager, common.RoleSystemAdmin},
148				expectedWrong: http.StatusForbidden,
149			},
150	
151			// --- Governance report endpoints ---
152			{
153				method: http.MethodPost,
154				path:   "/api/v1/governance/reports",
155				body: map[string]interface{}{
156					"target_type": common.ReportTargetTenant,
157					"target_id":   1,
158					"category":    "Harassment",
159					"description": "Matrix test report description that is sufficiently detailed for validation.",
160				},
161				// Any authenticated user can file a report.
162				allowedRoles:  []string{common.RoleTenant, common.RoleTechnician, common.RolePropertyManager, common.RoleComplianceReviewer, common.RoleSystemAdmin},
163				expectedWrong: http.StatusForbidden,
164			},
165			{
166				// Reading individual reports is restricted to ComplianceReviewer and SystemAdmin.
167				method:        http.MethodGet,
168				path:          fmt.Sprintf("/api/v1/governance/reports/%d", reportID),
169				body:          nil,
170				allowedRoles:  []string{common.RoleComplianceReviewer, common.RoleSystemAdmin},
171				expectedWrong: http.StatusForbidden,
172			},
173	
174			// --- Governance enforcement endpoints (ComplianceReviewer / SystemAdmin) ---
175			{
176				method:        http.MethodGet,
177				path:          "/api/v1/governance/enforcements",
178				body:          nil,
179				allowedRoles:  []string{common.RoleComplianceReviewer, common.RoleSystemAdmin},
180				expectedWrong: http.StatusForbidden,
181			},
182	
183			// --- Enforcement revoke ---
184			{
185				method: http.MethodPost,
186				path:   fmt.Sprintf("/api/v1/governance/enforcements/%d/revoke", enfID),
187				body: map[string]string{
188					"reason": "Matrix test revocation reason that is long enough for validation requirements.",
189				},
190				allowedRoles:  []string{common.RoleComplianceReviewer, common.RoleSystemAdmin},
191				expectedWrong: http.StatusForbidden,
192			},
193		}
194	
195		allRoles := []string{
196			common.RoleTenant,
197			common.RoleTechnician,
198			common.RolePropertyManager,
199			common.RoleComplianceReviewer,
200			common.RoleSystemAdmin,
201		}
202	
203		for _, tc := range cases {
204			tc := tc // capture range variable
205			t.Run(fmt.Sprintf("%s %s", tc.method, tc.path), func(t *testing.T) {
206				// Unauthenticated → always 401.
207				w := makeRequest(t, env.router, tc.method, tc.path, "", tc.body)
208				if w.Code != http.StatusUnauthorized {
209					t.Errorf("unauthenticated: expected 401, got %d; body: %s", w.Code, w.Body.String())
210				}
211	
212				// Build set of allowed roles for O(1) lookup.
213				allowed := make(map[string]bool, len(tc.allowedRoles))
214				for _, r := range tc.allowedRoles {
215					allowed[r] = true
216				}
217	
218				for _, role := range allRoles {
219					role := role
220					token := env.tokens[role]
221					w := makeRequest(t, env.router, tc.method, tc.path, token, tc.body)
222	
223					if allowed[role] {
224						// Correct role: must receive a 2xx response.
225						if w.Code < 200 || w.Code >= 300 {
226							t.Errorf("role=%s: expected 2xx (allowed), got %d; body: %s", role, w.Code, w.Body.String())
227						}
228					} else {
229						// Wrong role: should be 403 (or expectedWrong).
230						expectedCode := tc.expectedWrong
231						if expectedCode == 0 {
232							expectedCode = http.StatusForbidden
233						}
234						if w.Code != expectedCode && w.Code != http.StatusForbidden {
235							t.Errorf("role=%s: expected %d (denied), got %d; body: %s", role, expectedCode, w.Code, w.Body.String())
236						}
237					}
238				}
239			})
240		}
241	}

454	// property assignments receives an empty list from GET /payments, rather than leaking
455	// all payments.  Regression guard for the ScopedToPropertyIDs sentinel.

464		db.Exec("INSERT INTO property_staff_assignments (property_id, user_id, role, is_active) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)",
465			1, pm1User.ID, "PropertyManager", true)
466		pm1Token := loginUser(t, router, "scope_pm1", pm1Pw)

468		// PM2 has the PropertyManager role but no property assignments at all.

474			"property_id": 1,
475			"amount":      100.00,
476		})
477		assertStatus(t, w, http.StatusCreated)
478	
479		var createResp struct {
480			Data struct{ ID uint64 `json:"id"` } `json:"data"`
481		}
482		parseResponse(t, w, &createResp)
483		paymentID := createResp.Data.ID
484		if paymentID == 0 {
485			t.Fatalf("expected a non-zero payment ID from PM1")
486		}
487	
488		// PM2 lists payments — must get an empty list, not PM1's payment.
489		w = makeRequest(t, router, http.MethodGet, "/api/v1/payments", pm2Token, nil)
490		assertStatus(t, w, http.StatusOK)
491	
492		var listResp struct {
493			Data []struct{ ID uint64 `json:"id"` } `json:"data"`
494		}

502		if w.Code != http.StatusForbidden {

22	// setupTestDB opens a fresh in-memory SQLite database and auto-migrates all
23	// models required by the integration tests.
24	//
25	// IMPORTANT — DB fidelity caveat:
26	// SQLite + AutoMigrate does not reproduce MySQL-specific behaviors:
27	//   - JSON column semantics (MySQL JSON_TABLE, JSON_CONTAINS, etc.)
28	//   - Strict FK constraint enforcement and cascade ordering
29	//   - DECIMAL precision and rounding rules
30	//   - Index-level uniqueness enforcement differences
31	//
32	// For full schema/constraint fidelity, run integration tests against a real
33	// MySQL 8.0 instance:
34	//   TEST_MYSQL_DSN="user:pass@tcp(127.0.0.1:3306)/propertyops_test?parseTime=true" \
35	//     go test ./test/... -tags=mysql_integration
36	//
37	// If sqlite3 is unavailable (CGO_ENABLED=0 / no C compiler), the test is
38	// skipped with a clear message rather than failing.
39	func setupTestDB(t *testing.T) *gorm.DB {
40		t.Helper()
41	
42		// Each test gets its own private in-memory database so they never share state.
43		db, err := gorm.Open(sqlite.Open("file::memory:?mode=memory&cache=private"), &gorm.Config{
44			Logger:                                   logger.Discard,
45			DisableForeignKeyConstraintWhenMigrating: true,
46		})
47		if err != nil {
48			// gorm.io/driver/sqlite returns an error containing "CGO_ENABLED=0" or
49			// "cgo to work" when the binary was built without CGO support.
50			t.Skipf("SQLite unavailable (CGO disabled or no C compiler): %v", err)
51		}
52	
53		models := []interface{}{
54			// Auth
55			&authpkg.Session{},
56			&authpkg.User{},
57			&authpkg.Role{},
58			// Users
59			&userpkg.User{},
60			&userpkg.Role{},
61			&userpkg.UserRole{},
62			// Properties (required for PM scope checks against property_staff_assignments)
63			&properties.Property{},
64			&properties.Unit{},
65			&properties.PropertyStaffAssignment{},
66			&properties.TechnicianSkillTag{},
67			&properties.DispatchCursor{},
68			// Work Orders
69			&workorders.WorkOrder{},
70			&workorders.WorkOrderEvent{},
71			&workorders.CostItem{},
72			// Payments
73			&payments.Payment{},
74			&payments.PaymentApproval{},
75			&payments.ReconciliationRun{},
76			// Governance
77			&governance.Report{},
78			&governance.EnforcementAction{},
79			&governance.KeywordBlacklist{},
80			&governance.RiskRule{},
81			// Notifications
82			&notifications.Notification{},
83			&notifications.NotificationTemplate{},
84			&notifications.NotificationReceipt{},
85			&notifications.MessageThread{},
86			&notifications.ThreadParticipant{},
87			&notifications.ThreadMessage{},
88			// Audit
89			&audit.AuditLog{},
90		}
91	
92		if err := db.AutoMigrate(models...); err != nil {
93			t.Fatalf("AutoMigrate failed: %v", err)
94		}
95	
96		// SQLite does not enforce FK constraints by default; enable WAL for better
97		// concurrent-read performance in tests.
98		db.Exec("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL;")
99	
100		return db
101	}

136	func createTestUser(t *testing.T, db *gorm.DB, username, roleName string) (*authpkg.User, string) {

171			t.Fatalf("createTestUser: reload failed: %v", err)
172		}
